title: Art tuning workbench + web v2 round (hero pieces · live announcer · two-column)

content.objective: Tune the shipped procedural art with your eyes on the real generator output (comment per visual → parameter-sized PRs), and green-light the three deferred web-v2 items: hero pieces for class portraits/bosses, a live announcer via the local bridge, and the two-column desktop layout.
content.context: All 8 web-epic PRs (#85→#92) are reviewed (0 blocking) and awaiting your merge. The visuals below are rendered by the ACTUAL shipped generators (bundled from the epic branch — not mocks). Visual 1 is INTERACTIVE: every one of the 29 enemy portraits + 4 class emblems, with a live corruption slider (drag t from 0→1, or press "rank" to see each at its in-game base). Visual 2 shows the card frames per rarity + the size sweep the UI uses. Visual 3 is a layout sketch for the two-column desktop option. Every drawing dial already lives in exported params (STYLE for portraits, FRAME_STYLE for frames), so any note you leave ("towers need denser buses", "dim the motifs", "more corruption on bosses") becomes a small reviewed PR.

requirements.statement: Drawing-refinement rounds driven by your comments on the workbench visuals, shipped as parameter-sized PRs with before/after galleries.
requirements.rationale: You asked to keep refining the drawings; the dials exist (STYLE/FRAME_STYLE) — what's missing is your taste input on the real output.
acceptanceCriteria: each round: your notes → one PR touching params (+ snapshots regenerated deliberately)
before/after gallery attached to every round
no behavior/layout changes smuggled into art PRs

requirements.statement: Hero pieces: distinctive high-detail art for the 4 class portraits + 2 bosses, layered over the same frames/palette.
requirements.rationale: The procedural floor is consistent but capped; the 6 identity-defining pieces deserve more — sourcing is your call (open question below).
acceptanceCriteria: consistent with the glitch identity + theme tokens; procedural remains the fallback
licensing/provenance clean per the sourcing choice

requirements.statement: Live announcer: the local bridge exposes a narration endpoint so the browser can use the terminal-side Anthropic key to re-word lines; static remains default + fallback.
requirements.rationale: The web announcer ships hand-written; the terminal already supports live re-wording — bridging it keeps keys out of the browser and reuses the existing seam.
acceptanceCriteria: no API key ever reaches the browser (bridge-side only)
graceful: no key / no bridge → static lines, label says 'hand-written' vs 'live'
budget/rate limits mirrored from the terminal's dungeonAi

requirements.statement: Two-column desktop layout: at ≥960px a persistent left HUD rail beside the content column; current stack below that width.
requirements.rationale: The web playtest flagged dead viewport; C4's 62rem cap helped — the rail is the real fix for desktop, per the mock (visual 3).
acceptanceCriteria: left rail: HP/gold/depth, relics, potions, pile clock, announcer line; content right

content.design: TUNING LOOP: you comment on the visuals → I batch notes into parameter PRs (each with before/after galleries) → repeat until it looks right. HERO PIECES: bespoke high-detail art for the 4 class portraits + the 2 bosses — sourcing is the open question (procedural-hero mode = a richer generator path with more layers/detail budget for just those 6; commissioned human art ~$50-150; heavily-curated AI with disclosure). LIVE ANNOUNCER: the local save-bridge gains a narration endpoint so the browser can use your Anthropic key (terminal-side) to re-word the static lines — static stays the default/fallback. TWO-COLUMN: at ≥960px, a persistent left rail (HUD, relics, announcer, pile clock) beside the main content column — the mock shows the shape; mobile/narrow keeps the current stack.
openQuestions: TUNING: leave notes directly on visual 1 (portraits/emblems — drag the slider!) and visual 2 (frames). Anything from 'perfect, ship it' to per-family tweaks.

HERO PIECES sourcing: procedural-hero mode (a richer generator path, $0, stays on-brand) vs commissioned human art (~$50-150, best ceiling) vs heavily-curated AI with disclosure (fast, reputational tradeoff you know from the review)? My lean: procedural-hero first — if it doesn't wow, commission.
LIVE ANNOUNCER: default ON when a key exists terminal-side, or opt-in toggle?
TWO-COLUMN: go as mocked (visual 3), tweak the rail contents, or skip it?
